title: Lecture: “Why We Need Scores: From the Sumerians to Graphic Notation”
description: Starting from early notation systems, he traced how scores evolved over time and why they matter: not just technically, but culturally. The lecture helped position notation as something that shapes how we think about music itself.

title: Workshop: “Audible Spaces”
description: This workshop explored spatial hearing and its application in sound composition. Through theory, listening exercises, and hands-on work with SPASM for Live, participants created headphone-based sound environments, using movement and tracking to navigate sound in space. No prior experience was required.

title: Workshop: Intro to Set Design, Forms, Materials
Lecture: “What is Musical Performance”
description: This session questioned what performance actually is: moving beyond execution of a score towards presence, interpretation, and the relationship between body, sound, and space.

title: Lecture: “Introduction to Compositional Strategies of Notation and Scores”
description: Spoke about notation as something fluid: not only a way to fix music, but a way to propose it. The lecture focused on open scores, graphic notation, and systems where performers become co-authors.
